description_long: This object is a small airplane with a light tan fuselage and a pair of straight wings extending from either side. The wings are positioned low on the body, and the tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage, with a clear canopy or window area visible. The airplane is surrounded by green vegetation and appears to be situated in a landscaped area, possibly near a house or garden. The overall appearance suggests a general aviation aircraft, likely used for private or recreational flying.
description: A small tan airplane with low-mounted straight wings, a conventional tail, and a forward cockpit, set amidst green landscaping.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from an oblique angle, showing the tan fuselage, low wings, and the surrounding green vegetation.
description_view_1: This view highlights the front and one side of the airplane, with the cockpit and nose clearly visible among the greenery.
description_view_2: A distant, side-on view showing the airplane's profile, wingspan, and the tail section, with vegetation partially obscuring details.
description_view_3: The rear and opposite side of the airplane are visible, with the tail and horizontal stabilizers prominent, and the cockpit area facing away.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with fixed main and nose gear units
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy or window area
